speaker: interviewee
text: This is a group of survivors.

speaker: interviewer
text: The top.

speaker: interviewee
text: It's a group of survivors, which--

speaker: interviewer
text: You have to do the top one because he's focused on it.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a group, where we went in 1945 to Treblinka with the historic committee, under the supervision from Dr. Friedman. In this picture, the names which I can remember is [NON-ENGLISH]. And, I really don't remember these names, because I didn't see these people already since 1945. So I don't know. This is-- he is a Canadian. He lived in Canada. This is Dr-- [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] I-- I forgot that name from him. And this is-- yeah, I forgot that name from them.

speaker: interviewer
text: This is [NON-ENGLISH]. This is the survivors. OK. One moment. He'll focus on it, and then you'll talk about it.

speaker: interviewee
text: All right.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is-- this group, we are five survivors from Treblinka. Their names-- I will-- I don't remember the names. I know one guy from here is in South-- South America. I'm the first-- I'm the second one from the--

speaker: interviewer
text: Left.

speaker: interviewee
text: From the left. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] There-- the next to me, he is in South America. I don't know the name.

speaker: interviewer
text: When was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in 1945, on that ground-- on the ground of Treblinka, at that place where Treblinka was there.

speaker: interviewer
text: And which of the-- which Treblinka was it?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the Treblinka that burned, and they have the crematoriums.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK. Is this the same?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is-- this is a group, which was-- were also in Treblinka. Oh, but-- all these pictures, what I show it to you, this was taken out from a magazine which appeared in Israel during the Eichmann trial. I'm here, on the-- on the right side. And [NON-ENGLISH] is here. And the-- the man in the military uniform, he was from Yad Vashem. I don't know the name from him.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is from Treblinka. We found these bones on the ground of Treblinka, when we were there in 1945. Was all over, laying around-- bones on the field. We picked it up, some of them, and we made a picture out of it.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: The picture on the right is Fred [NON-ENGLISH]. He was liberated-- we liberated ourselves, and this was in 1944. He lives now in California. He's a very comfortable person. And he gave a $1 million to the museum in Washington.

speaker: interviewer
text: And he made the diagram?

speaker: interviewee
text: He made, yeah. He made the-- he made that diagram from Treblinka. This picture is taken in Stuttgart in the DP camp. One morning, the German police came. They accused something-- then the Jews there are doing some business with-- with-- with-- out of-- out of the camp. So they shot somebody. They shot a man with that name Danziger-- Danziger was shot. Then we called a meeting to protest the shooting in that DP camp. And I'm talking at that time to the people about the whole incident.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the date-- do you know the date?

speaker: interviewee
text: The date-- this is 1946, in February.

speaker: interviewer
text: Good.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is in Stuttgart before I left to go to America. In the picture is here-- I'm there. My wife is there. And my wife's brother and sister-in-law are saying goodbye to us before we left to the port to go to Stuttgart.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. You're on the far right, and your wife is on the far left?

speaker: interviewee
text: The left, yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the brother-in-law and sister-in-law are in the middle.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. This picture was taken in New York already. This is-- I'm here with my wife and my two daughters. If I'm not mistaken, this was taken in 19-- 50-- in '52, '53, '52.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Which daughter-- which daughter is on-- in the corner there?

speaker: interviewee
text: This? [LAUGHTER] That's a little-- yeah. This is Sheila, this is my wife, I am, that's Debbie.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken when the children were small. And I enlarged it. My daughter enlarged it from a small, little picture. And here am I and my wife, Helen.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the year?

speaker: interviewee
text: The year when that was taken-- the picture was taken, oh-- [LAUGHS] '50-- this picture was taken in-- this was [NON-ENGLISH]. That's right, it's when the-- already, this is '47 or '48.

speaker: interviewer
text: Very good.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture is taken at a party from Ben Gurion Club. Here is the former president of the Ben Gurion Club giving me a citation as being on the board of the Ben Gurion Club.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] '78-- '78-- about '81, '82. This is the citation which I received in the previous picture we saw. The president from the Ben Gurion Club gave it to me. This picture was taken at a Yom Hashoah gathering here in Florida. I was the speaker that time from-- about Yom Hashoah. I was vice president from the Holocaust Survivors of South Florida. This citation I received for-- for good work, as vice president for the Holocaust Survivors of South Florida.

speaker: interviewer
text: When?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's '85, '84, this. This picture was taken in '83, '84. The ship's the I was the chairman from the group where we went for a cruise.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mr. Friedman, can you--

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in mine-- our 50th anniversary, our wedding anniversary on the-- on the ground of the Palmier Hotel in Florida. And the picture here is-- I'm there, I, myself; my wife, Helen; my daughter, Debbie; her husband, Kenny; her daughter, Stacey; and her daughter, Ivy. And the other one is my daughter, Sheila; my son-in-law, Dick' my granddaughter, Carrie, and my grandson, Mark.

speaker: interviewer
text: Good.
